mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2017 payroll growth at 146,730k persons indicates sustained labor market strength
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k persons.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year, the Fed maintains a restrictive real rate stance that suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2017 FEDFUNDS at 1.16% and CPIAUCSL at 245.03 index
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2017 GDPC1 at 17030.0 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strengthening consumer demand and labor market tightness, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through higher corporate revenue and pricing power expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2017 RSAFS at 474,841 million dollars and UNRATE at 4.4%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production (INDPRO) growth below 0.1% signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 62% of months where INDPRO growth is below 0.1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% year-over-year signals weakening residential investment and forward consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction and related sectors face earnings headwinds.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 65% of periods where HOUST declines exceed 10% YoY, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.5% while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2.0%, the Fed faces a policy bind that constrains future rate cuts, reducing equity duration and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2017 UNRATE at 4.4% and FEDFUNDS at 1.16%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are simultaneously met.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2017 UNRATE at 4.4% represents stable labor market conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of months where UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
